title: WIDTH: propagation through arithmetic
body:
This is the first one towards #2433 
I have seen this quite often and it is kind of common with parameters:
```
module t;

  localparam logic[3:0] A = 4'h0;
  localparam B = 46 + A;
  localparam logic[12:0] C = 46 + A;

endmodule
```
Leads to:
```
%Warning-WIDTH: t/t_issue-2433.v:4:21: Operator ADD expects 32 or 6 bits on the RHS, but RHS's VARREF 'A' generates 4 bits.
                                     : ... In instance t
    4 |   localparam B = 46 + A;
      |                     ^
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Warning-WIDTH: t/t_issue-2433.v:5:33: Operator ADD expects 32 or 13 bits on the RHS, but RHS's VARREF 'A' generates 4 bits.
                                     : ... In instance t
    5 |   localparam logic[12:0] C = 46 + A;
      |                                 ^
```
For line 4:
 - (1800-2017 11.6.1) size of the add is max(size(46)=6, size(A)=4)=6
 - (1800-2017 11.8.2) propagate back size 6 to 46 and A.
 - (1800-2017 11.8.3) RHS=6, LHS/context=32 -> RHS=32

I looked at `V3Width` before when I tried to solve #2070. It seems kind of in the same area (up- and down-propagating). It uses the min/max sizes (thats where the 32 comes from) and I believe there is an issue when back-propagating from assignment to the ADD.
For line 5 it should be the same, but only with context=13->RHS=13.
Either way, my understanding from the spec is that both statements are actually okay, or am I missing something?
This removes the warning by the way:
```
module t;

  localparam logic[3:0] A = 4'h0;
  localparam B = 46 + 32'(A);
  localparam logic[12:0] C = 46 + 13'(A);

endmodule
```
